feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAC reports to live HANA prompting for user name and password
entry.content: we have SAC reports which is configured with HANA live connection, reports keeps asking for hana user and password, please advise how can we avoid the prompt

entry.title: SAC calculated measure ( with 0 instead off null) in filter panel doesn't work
entry.content:
Measure ( REV_CY) has rows with null value and , as I cannot filter it , I created a new measure where write 0 instead to null.
Is there a reason why I continue to not see the lines with 0 value calculated ?

 

Lines with "VAL FATT AC" equal ro null but copied in  REV_CY_FILTER wiht 0 are not showed

tks

entry.title: How to make story NOT planning enabled by default
entry.content:
Stories created by users on a planning model are set to planning enabled by default.

We have a requirement where a Team of users need the ability to create reporting stories on a planning model. The Team members that are not Planning users cannot publish any data in these stories as expected, however users that have planning roles are able to publish data in the user created stories, as the story is "Planning Enabled" when created. We are looking for a way to ensure the user created stories on a planning model are not able to be used to enter data by a planning user. The planning users do enter data in “authorised” planning stories as part of the planning process.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Duration calculation Between Two Dates
entry.content: I have employee event history data with multiple records for the event reason “Promotion - Annual Pay Change” for the same employee. I need to calculate the duration between the latest Start Date of the promotion event and the Last End date or till now. Since there are multiple promotion records, I want SAC to dynamically pick the most recent Start Date and calculate the duration.

entry.title: How to display multiple measures in SAC pie/donut chart?
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

It seems that only one measure is allowed for SAC pie/donut chart according to https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/41836ac6c47d4a96987622a7ecc422b0.html?q=pie+chart#donut-or-pie-charts.

I am wondering whether there is some workaround to do so....as I have three measures and would like to combine their total in one pie/donut chart. A screenshot has been attached for illustration.

Any tips or guidance would be highly appreciated! Thank you in advance.

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: S4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD CDS VIEWS for FP&A Planning
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are currently implementing SAC FP&A Planning for our client, involving the following system landscape: S4HANA Public Cloud, Datasphere, and SAC for Planning. The planning scenarios we are focusing on include Opex, Capex, Revenue, and P&L.

We have established the following communication arrangements:

  • SAP_COM_0002
  • SAP_COM_0008
  • SAP_COM_0532
  • SAP_COM_0065
  • SAP_COM_0087

However, we are facing challenges in identifying the appropriate CDS views for transaction data. I have attempted to utilize the following CDS views from the SAP portal, but they are unavailable for extraction:

  • I_JournalEntryItem → Full line-item detail
  • I_ActualPlanJournalEntryItem → Actual + Plan
  • I_GLAccountLineItemCube → Optimized analytical line items

Could you please advise if there are any communication arrangements we may have overlooked or how we can enable data extraction? Additionally, I would appreciate a list of CDS views we should use for both Master data and Transaction data to effectively carry out the FP&A Planning, espe

entry.title: Unable to call Parameterized ODATA services in the build query panel in SAC
entry.content:
We have custom CDS query (ODATA) which has parameters and we need to access that in SAC as an import data source.
Business doesn't want to use freehand query please advise how to achieve this.

Thanks
Arjun

entry.title: Establish Connection Between SAC and On-Premise HANA DB
entry.content:
We want to Establish the Connection Between SAC and On-Premise HANA DB. We are referred the below link. We are getting below error. 

We couldn't connect to your HANA system. For more information, see our troubleshooting page.TroubleshootCorrelation ID: 30303874649147778769255325863782Copy to Clipboard

Live Data Connection to SAP HANA On-Premise Using a Tunnel Connection | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Parent-Child Drill-Down Hierarchy from S/4 CDS View to SAC Live Connection
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to develop a Parent-Child drill-down hierarchy in an SAP S/4HANA CDS View and then consume this hierarchy in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) using a Live Connection.

I have already tried creating the hierarchy on the CDS side, and the hierarchy is visible in SAC, but it does not behave like a proper drill-down hierarchy when consumed through the live connection.

Could anyone please confirm:

  • Is it possible to create a Parent-Child drill-down hierarchy completely on the CDS side and consume it in SAC via Live Connection?
  • Are there any specific annotations, hierarchy definitions, or SAC limitations that need to be considered?
  • Has anyone successfully implemented this scenario?

Any guidance or sample approach would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Role based security at parent node in SAC
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have role based security enabled and want to provide access at the parent level (parent child hierarchy). When we are trying to select Hierarchy and give access at parent node, it does not allow to select the parent node but at flat presentation, it does allow to select the parent but then data cannot be seen in the templates. Role based security does not allow to select the parent node or hierarchy node?

Thanks & Regards,

Pranav Vyas

entry.title: SAC Calendar time to complete actions in Parallel
entry.content:
hi,

We have noticed that when 2 Action (Multi Action or Data Action) run at the same time, both will take a lot more time to complete than if they run in sequence (one after the other).

This happens even if each is impacting a different models (i.e. one updating FINANCE and one updating REV_PLANNING).

The same task running in concurrence (at the same time as other task) may take more than to times more to complete.

Is there any way to manage parallel jobs in SAC?

Can we schedule 2 tasks to run at the same time in the calendar and not have this huge impact on time to complete?

entry.title: SAC: Measure-Based Dimension vs Calculated Dimension
entry.content:
Hi everyone 

I have this situation:

In Customer public dimension each customer has longitude and latitude coordinate.

A Geo Map (Bubble) is done using this dimension (Customer Geo)

Bubble color is with "revenue" measure.

All works well and customers are in the map

Now I would like to show on the map only customers more than  50k revenue (e.g)

Which is the best way to  achieve this goal ?

tks

entry.title: Dimension load is failing in SAP SAC
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

Where can I see the root cause of the data load failure for a master data object in SAP SAC. I don't see any info related to this failure. Could you please help

entry.title: SAC Planning – Show ACTUAL and WORKING in Same Table with Editable WORKING Only
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a requirement in SAP Analytics Cloud Planning.

Current setup:

  • Rows = Account

  • Columns = Version (ACTUAL, WORKING)

Requirement:

  • Both ACTUAL and WORKING versions should be visible in the same table.

  • ACTUAL should be read-only.

  • WORKING should be editable.

Issue:
I tried using an Input Control on the Version dimension and selected only WORKING. However, the Input Control filters the entire table widget, so ACTUAL gets removed from the table.

Is there any standard approach or best practice in SAC to:

  • Display both ACTUAL and WORKING in the same table

  • Keep ACTUAL read-only

  • Allow editing only for WORKING

Any suggestions would be appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: E-Mail Notification is not working
entry.content:
Hi Team,

In the SAC optimised story, we tried to send the report through an email upon clicking of button.

It is working with manually "Schedule Publication". But, we want to trigger based on certain event in the story.

Please suggest what are options available in SAC.

entry.title: SAC disable does not work from API
entry.content: "We need SCIM API write access to deactivate users via PATCH /api/v1/scim/Users. Our API Access OAuth client with 'User Provisioning' scope only has read access (sap.fpa.user). How do we enable the write scope for SCIM user deactivation as described in the Q4 2024 release?"

entry.title: Data Actions on Formula Account Fails in DEV but works in PRD
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have the following accounts:

And when I try to execute the following copy step, it fails (it started failing this week, before it was working ok, no changes were made):

If i run it in PRD, it works normal. I guess is something related to the tenant capacity or a parameter, but I've tried to scope a minimal data region and it still fails. 

Of course I could use other strategies like removing the formulas and re-doing the calculations in DA, but this is a live implementation with a lot of those formulas, and it won't be feasible to do it.

Any ideas of what could be happening? 

Thanks

entry.title: [SAC Planning] Is it realistic to operate a “Category” dimension with up to ~50,000 members created
entry.content:
1. What I would like to ask

In a SAC Planning model, we are considering a design where we create a Category dimension whose members are derived from a combination of three keys, resulting in approximately 5,000 to up to 50,000 unique keys.
Is it generally realistic/acceptable in SAC to define and operate a dimension of this size, from both a performance and operational perspective? If not recommended, what alternative design approaches would you suggest?

2. Assumptions / Environment

  • Product: SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
  • Model type: Planning model
  • Target dimension: Category dimension (with a hierarchy)
  • Number of members: approx. 5,000 to max. ~50,000 unique keys
  • Story consumption:
    • We do not expand or display all 50,000 members in charts/tables
    • The actual number of rows displayed in a table is around 100
  • The set of unique keys is fixed (not expected to grow over time)

3. Background (data integration scenario)

We use a Data Action to transfer data from another model (source) into the target planning model.
The target model has an additional analysis axis: the Category dimension, which does not exist in the source model.

Right after the data transfer, since Category is not provided in the source, the facts are initially posted to “Unassigned” for the Category dimension.
After that, we plan to run a Data Action to re-allocate (expand) the “Unassigned” facts into the appropriate Category members, and then delete the remaining “Unassigned” facts.

4. Proposed design (mapping approach)

  • There are three common key dimensions that exist in both the source and target models.
  • In the Category dimension, each member stores the values of these three keys as properties.
  • We are considering treating the combination of the three keys as a unique identifier, and using that combined key as the Category dimension member ID (or an equivalent key).
  • In the Data Action, we allocate “Unassigned” facts to the appropriate Category member based on the stored property values (the three keys), and then delete the “Unassigned” facts.

5. Concerns / Questions

  1. Is having a Category dimension with around 50,000 members generally within an acceptable and manageable range for SAC Planning models?
  2. If performance issues are likely, which areas are typically most impacted?
    • e.g., Data Action runtime, write/delete operations, hierarchy processing, value help/search, model saving/publishing, etc.
  3. Is a design where a 3-key combination becomes the Category member ID recommended?
    • If not, what are common alternatives in SAC (e.g., mapping via attributes, bridge-like approach, pre-aggregation, deriving category on the source side, etc.)?
  4. Are there any best practices for operations?
    • e.g., scoping Data Actions, precautions for deletions, hierarchy design, naming/ID design, etc.

6. Additional note

  • The unique keys (category candidates) are fixed and will not grow over time.
  • Users will not browse or display the full set of 50,000 members (typical display is ~100 rows).

entry.title: Custom CSS is now missing in SAC stories???
entry.content:
I have an existing story that used a lot of custom CSS - today when editing the story the CSS button is missing  & and there's no CSS selector in the styling panel for any of the widgets?

what on earth is going on ? why was this removed? 
A lot of my stories use custom CSS and being unable to edit it etc is a massive problem.

Anyone else seeing this issue?

entry.title: Sap analytical cloud free trail to more 30 days
entry.content:
Hi All,

I would like to know that if I need to extent days of my Sap analytical cloud free trail to more 30 days then i cant see any option in my tool, it already been nearly to expire now I need for extending, kindly help me and assist how to resolve.

Thanks,

kanchan
